text.title: finops-devops-cost-kubernetes Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for finops-devops-cost-kubernetes. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Replace single-pool Kubernetes clusters with at minimum three node pools: cpu-intensive (compute-optimized: c2/c3 on GCP, c5/c6 on AWS), memory-intensive (high-memory: n2-highmem on GCP, r5/r6 on AWS), and general (cost-optimized: e2 on GCP, m5/t3 on AWS). Enable Cluster Autoscaler on all pools. Configure PodAffinity or NodeSelector to route workloads to matching pools.
rule.rationale: GKE benchmark: 500 uniform n2-standard-8 nodes at 25% CPU → 280 nodes across workload-specific pools at 55% CPU. Monthly savings: $57,600 (48%). The split allows each pool to autoscale independently based on its actual workload type.

rule.statement: Create a dedicated Spot node pool for batch, ML training, and CI/CD workloads. Taint the pool with `cloud.google.com/gke-spot=true:NoSchedule` (GCP) or `kubernetes.io/arch=spot:NoSchedule` (EKS Spot). Add corresponding Toleration to batch job specs. Ensure batch jobs have pod disruption budgets and restart policies set to OnFailure to handle preemption gracefully.
rule.rationale: The taint ensures services never land on the Spot pool accidentally. The toleration ensures batch jobs explicitly opt in. OnFailure restart policy handles the 2-minute preemption notice by allowing the scheduler to replace the pod on an On-Demand node if no Spot capacity is available.

rule.statement: Deploy VPA in Recommendation mode on all deployments for 7 days before any other optimization. Review VPA recommendations for pods where current requests exceed actual usage by 50%+ — these are the highest-impact rightsizing candidates. Enable VPA Auto mode only on stateless deployments (not databases, not single-replica stateful sets) after validating Recommendation mode for 7 days.
rule.rationale: Recommendation mode shows the gap without risk. Auto mode restarts pods to apply new requests — acceptable for stateless services, potentially disruptive for stateful ones. 7-day observation captures weekly traffic patterns before committing to new limits.

rule.statement: After 30 days of running workload-specific node pools with autoscaling, identify the stable baseline: the minimum node count observed over the 30-day window for each pool. Purchase 1-year Committed Use Discounts covering 80% of this baseline. Leave 20% on On-Demand to absorb measurement error. Review and adjust commitments quarterly.
rule.rationale: CUDs commit to vCPU and memory amounts, not specific instance types — more flexible than VM-level RIs. Purchasing after 30 days of autoscaling ensures the baseline reflects actual workload requirements post-optimization, not the original over-provisioned size.
